ref: Noah did everything God told him to do.
text: Nowa yaa nye dii meena ti Wen weeni wa la.
translated: Nowa is going to the market to buy fish.

ref: But God answered: No! You and Sarah will have a son. His name will be Isaac, and I will make an everlasting promise to him and his descendants.
text: Wen yaa weeni Abrayam ayen, m siak ya, alege fi powa Saara ale biak nidoabili ate fi te wa yuei ayen Aizik. M le basi ate mi ale wa nɔiyeŋka a zaani ate ka chim nɔiyeŋ kaai ale kaa kpeglim a te wa ŋaaŋbisaŋa ale bo wa ŋaaŋ cheen la.
translated: When Abraham was old, the Lord appeared to him and said, "I will bless Sarah, and she will bear you a son, and you shall call his name Isaac. I will establish my covenant with him as an everlasting covenant for his descendants after him."

ref: Jacob cut branches from some poplar trees and from some almond and evergreen trees. He peeled off part of the bark and made the branches look spotted and speckled.
text: Jekob yaa pai niak tiib ale almond tiib ale dapeelita tiib naŋ yɔgsisa ate ba che la a po, a yaa pai daataŋa ate wa poa ate ŋa peenti la a dueni peelim.
translated: Jacob went to the market to buy almonds, dates, and dried apricots. He wanted to make a special dessert for his family.

ref: As Jacob was on his way back home, some of God's angels came and met him.
text: Jekob mɛ yaa lɔ wa suik po a cheŋ, ate Wen maarɔma yaa tu wa suik.
translated: Jacob is going to the farm to get some food, but Wen is going to the market to get some food.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Dila Wen yaa nye nuru ase wadek la, wadek ale ka diila ate wa nye wa, nidoa ale nipok ate wa nye.
translated: I am going to tell you a story, a story that happened to me, listen carefully to what I am going to say.

ref: Noah did everything God told him to do.
text: Nowa yaa nye dii meena ti Wen weeni wa la.
translated: Nowa is going to the market to buy meat.

ref: But God answered: No! You and Sarah will have a son. His name will be Isaac, and I will make an everlasting promise to him and his descendants.
text: Wen yaa weeni Abrayam ayen, m siak ya, alege fi powa Saara ale biak nidoabili ate fi te wa yuei ayen Aizik. M le basi ate mi ale wa nɔiyeŋka a zaani ate ka chim nɔiyeŋ kaai ale kaa kpeglim a te wa ŋaaŋbisaŋa ale bo wa ŋaaŋ cheen la.
translated: When Abraham was old, his wife Sarah was also old. God had promised Sarah that she would have a child, but she was too old to have a child. God kept his promise and Sarah had a son named Isaac. God blessed Isaac and gave him many descendants.

ref: Jacob cut branches from some poplar trees and from some almond and evergreen trees. He peeled off part of the bark and made the branches look spotted and speckled.
text: Jekob yaa pai niak tiib ale almond tiib ale dapeelita tiib naŋ yɔgsisa ate ba che la a po, a yaa pai daataŋa ate wa poa ate ŋa peenti la a dueni peelim.
translated: Jacob went to buy almonds, dates, and dried figs. He wanted to make a cake, but he didn't have enough flour.

ref: As Jacob was on his way back home, some of God's angels came and met him.
text: Jekob mɛ yaa lɔ wa suik po a cheŋ, ate Wen maarɔma yaa tu wa suik.
translated: Jacob is going to buy some fish, but Wen wants to buy some too.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Dila Wen yaa nye nuru ase wadek la, wadek ale ka diila ate wa nye wa, nidoa ale nipok ate wa nye.
translated: The sun is setting, the birds are returning to their nests, and the children are coming home from playing.

ref: Noah did everything God told him to do.
text: Nowa yaa nye dii meena ti Wen weeni wa la.
translated: Nowa is going to the market to buy fish.

ref: But God answered: No! You and Sarah will have a son. His name will be Isaac, and I will make an everlasting promise to him and his descendants.
text: Wen yaa weeni Abrayam ayen, m siak ya, alege fi powa Saara ale biak nidoabili ate fi te wa yuei ayen Aizik. M le basi ate mi ale wa nɔiyeŋka a zaani ate ka chim nɔiyeŋ kaai ale kaa kpeglim a te wa ŋaaŋbisaŋa ale bo wa ŋaaŋ cheen la.
translated: When Abraham was old, his wife Sarah was also old. God had promised Abraham that Sarah would have a son, and she did give birth to Isaac. I will bless her and also give her many descendants, so that her descendants will be as numerous as the stars in the sky and as the sand on the seashore.

ref: Jacob cut branches from some poplar trees and from some almond and evergreen trees. He peeled off part of the bark and made the branches look spotted and speckled.
text: Jekob yaa pai niak tiib ale almond tiib ale dapeelita tiib naŋ yɔgsisa ate ba che la a po, a yaa pai daataŋa ate wa poa ate ŋa peenti la a dueni peelim.
translated: Jacob went to the market to buy almonds, dates, and dried figs. He bought a lot of dates and figs, but only a little bit of almonds.

ref: As Jacob was on his way back home, some of God's angels came and met him.
text: Jekob mɛ yaa lɔ wa suik po a cheŋ, ate Wen maarɔma yaa tu wa suik.
translated: Jacob is going to the farm to plant corn, but Wen is going to the farm to harvest corn.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Dila Wen yaa nye nuru ase wadek la, wadek ale ka diila ate wa nye wa, nidoa ale nipok ate wa nye.
translated: I am going to the farm to work, I will come back when I finish, I will bring some food for you.

ref: Noah did everything God told him to do.
text: Nowa yaa nye dii meena ti Wen weeni wa la.
translated: Nowa is going to the market to buy some fish.

ref: But God answered: No! You and Sarah will have a son. His name will be Isaac, and I will make an everlasting promise to him and his descendants.
text: Wen yaa weeni Abrayam ayen, m siak ya, alege fi powa Saara ale biak nidoabili ate fi te wa yuei ayen Aizik. M le basi ate mi ale wa nɔiyeŋka a zaani ate ka chim nɔiyeŋ kaai ale kaa kpeglim a te wa ŋaaŋbisaŋa ale bo wa ŋaaŋ cheen la.
translated: When Abraham was old, his wife Sarah was also old. God had promised Abraham that Sarah would have a son, and she did have a son when she was very old. Abraham named him Isaac. God blessed Abraham in many ways, and he gave him many possessions, including flocks of sheep and goats, and many servants.

ref: Jacob cut branches from some poplar trees and from some almond and evergreen trees. He peeled off part of the bark and made the branches look spotted and speckled.
text: Jekob yaa pai niak tiib ale almond tiib ale dapeelita tiib naŋ yɔgsisa ate ba che la a po, a yaa pai daataŋa ate wa poa ate ŋa peenti la a dueni peelim.
translated: Jacob went to buy almonds, dates, and dried figs. He wanted to make a cake for his mother, who was sick.

ref: As Jacob was on his way back home, some of God's angels came and met him.
text: Jekob mɛ yaa lɔ wa suik po a cheŋ, ate Wen maarɔma yaa tu wa suik.
translated: Jacob is going to the farm to plant corn, but Wen is going to the farm to harvest corn.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Dila Wen yaa nye nuru ase wadek la, wadek ale ka diila ate wa nye wa, nidoa ale nipok ate wa nye.
translated: The one who is called Wen, he is not good, he is bad, he does not want to listen to us, he does not want to help us.

ref: Noah did everything God told him to do.
text: Nowa yaa nye dii meena ti Wen weeni wa la.
translated: Nowa is going to give meena to Wen weeni.

ref: But God answered: No! You and Sarah will have a son. His name will be Isaac, and I will make an everlasting promise to him and his descendants.
text: Wen yaa weeni Abrayam ayen, m siak ya, alege fi powa Saara ale biak nidoabili ate fi te wa yuei ayen Aizik. M le basi ate mi ale wa nɔiyeŋka a zaani ate ka chim nɔiyeŋ kaai ale kaa kpeglim a te wa ŋaaŋbisaŋa ale bo wa ŋaaŋ cheen la.
translated: When Abraham was old, his wife Sarah was also old. God had promised Abraham that Sarah would have a son, and she did give birth to Isaac. But Sarah was afraid that Isaac would be killed because he was the only son. So she told Abraham to take Isaac and go to a place where no one knew them.

ref: Jacob cut branches from some poplar trees and from some almond and evergreen trees. He peeled off part of the bark and made the branches look spotted and speckled.

translated: Jacob went to the market to buy almonds, dates, and dried figs. He bought a lot of dates and figs, and a little bit of almonds.

ref: As Jacob was on his way back home, some of God's angels came and met him.
text: Jekob mɛ yaa lɔ wa suik po a cheŋ, ate Wen maarɔma yaa tu wa suik.
translated: Jacob is going to the farm to get some food, but Wen is going to the farm too.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Dila Wen yaa nye nuru ase wadek la, wadek ale ka diila ate wa nye wa, nidoa ale nipok ate wa nye.
translated: I am going to the farm to work, I will come back when I finish, don't worry about me.

ref: Noah did everything God told him to do.
text: Nowa yaa nye dii meena ti Wen weeni wa la.
translated: Nowa is going to bring meena to Wen weeni's house.

ref: But God answered: No! You and Sarah will have a son. His name will be Isaac, and I will make an everlasting promise to him and his descendants.
text: Wen yaa weeni Abrayam ayen, m siak ya, alege fi powa Saara ale biak nidoabili ate fi te wa yuei ayen Aizik. M le basi ate mi ale wa nɔiyeŋka a zaani ate ka chim nɔiyeŋ kaai ale kaa kpeglim a te wa ŋaaŋbisaŋa ale bo wa ŋaaŋ cheen la.
translated: When Abraham was old, his wife Sarah was barren and unable to conceive. But God promised him that Sarah would bear a son, and she did give birth to Isaac. He circumcised him on the eighth day, as God had commanded him.

ref: Jacob cut branches from some poplar trees and from some almond and evergreen trees. He peeled off part of the bark and made the branches look spotted and speckled.
text: Jekob yaa pai niak tiib ale almond tiib ale dapeelita tiib naŋ yɔgsisa ate ba che la a po, a yaa pai daataŋa ate wa poa ate ŋa peenti la a dueni peelim.
translated: Jacob went to buy almonds, dates, and dried apricots. He wanted to make a cake, but he didn't have enough flour.

ref: As Jacob was on his way back home, some of God's angels came and met him.
text: Jekob mɛ yaa lɔ wa suik po a cheŋ, ate Wen maarɔma yaa tu wa suik.
translated: Jacob is going to the farm to get some food, but Wen is also going to the farm.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Dila Wen yaa nye nuru ase wadek la, wadek ale ka diila ate wa nye wa, nidoa ale nipok ate wa nye.
translated: I am going to the farm to work, I will come back when I finish, I will bring some food for you.

ref: Noah did everything God told him to do.
text: Nowa yaa nye dii meena ti Wen weeni wa la.
translated: Nowa is going to give meena to Wen weeni's mother.

ref: But God answered: No! You and Sarah will have a son. His name will be Isaac, and I will make an everlasting promise to him and his descendants.
text: Wen yaa weeni Abrayam ayen, m siak ya, alege fi powa Saara ale biak nidoabili ate fi te wa yuei ayen Aizik. M le basi ate mi ale wa nɔiyeŋka a zaani ate ka chim nɔiyeŋ kaai ale kaa kpeglim a te wa ŋaaŋbisaŋa ale bo wa ŋaaŋ cheen la.
translated: When Abraham was old, his wife Sarah was also old. God had promised them a son, but Sarah was barren and unable to conceive. So, Abraham took Hagar, Sarah's maidservant, as a wife. Hagar conceived and bore a son named Ishmael. But God had a plan for Abraham and Sarah to have a son of their own, Isaac.

ref: Jacob cut branches from some poplar trees and from some almond and evergreen trees. He peeled off part of the bark and made the branches look spotted and speckled.
text: Jekob yaa pai niak tiib ale almond tiib ale dapeelita tiib naŋ yɔgsisa ate ba che la a po, a yaa pai daataŋa ate wa poa ate ŋa peenti la a dueni peelim.
translated: Jacob went to the market to buy almonds, dates, and dried mangoes. He bought a lot of them, and he went home to eat them with his family.

ref: As Jacob was on his way back home, some of God's angels came and met him.
text: Jekob mɛ yaa lɔ wa suik po a cheŋ, ate Wen maarɔma yaa tu wa suik.
translated: Jacob went to the farm to work, but Wen stayed home to cook.
